question: P alone can complete a piece of work in 15 days and Q alone in 20 days. P and Q undertake to do the work for ₹ 4,500. With the help of R, they complete the work in 6 days. How much should be paid to R?
option_1: ₹1200

option_3: ₹1500
option_4: ₹1800
solution_text:
Total work = LCM of 15 and 20 = 60 units

P = 60/15 = 4 units/day
Q = 60/20 = 3 units/day
P + Q + R = 60/6 = 10 units/day

R = 10 - 4 - 3 = 3 units/day

R’s 6 days work = 3 × 6 = 18 units

R’s share = 18/60 × 4500 = ₹1350

question:
Read the following passage and answer the given questions accordingly:

The intellectual lineage of Ancient Greece reached its zenith through the triumvirate of Socrates, Plato, and Aristotle. Socrates, the foundational figure, operated on the premise that "the unexamined life is not worth living," employing a dialectic method to strip away false certainties. Unlike the Sophists who charged for wisdom, Socrates claimed to know nothing, viewing himself merely as a "midwife" to the ideas of others. His trial and subsequent execution for impiety and corrupting the youth marked a pivotal moment in Western thought, transforming him into a martyr for the cause of rational inquiry and moral integrity.

Plato, Socrates' most illustrious student, preserved his mentor’s legacy while expanding into metaphysical realms. In his "Theory of Forms," Plato posited that the physical world is but a shadowy reflection of a higher, immutable reality of perfect ideas. Aristotle, however, shifted this focus back to the empirical world. As a student at Plato’s Academy, he eventually diverged from his teacher’s idealism to champion "Empiricism"—the belief that knowledge is derived from sensory experience and logical categorization. While Plato looked toward the heavens for truth, Aristotle looked toward the earth, laying the groundwork for modern scientific methodology.

Q- According to the passage, what was the primary difference between Socrates and the Sophists?
option_1: Socrates utilized the dialectic method while the Sophists used logic.
option_2: Socrates charged high fees whereas the Sophists taught for free.
option_3: Socrates focused on the youth whereas the Sophists focused on adults.
option_4: Socrates did not charge for wisdom whereas the Sophists were paid.
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Socrates did not charge for wisdom whereas the Sophists were paid.

Explanation:

  • The passage highlights that Socrates claimed to know nothing and did not charge for wisdom, instead acting as a "midwife" to help others discover ideas.
  • In contrast, the Sophists charged fees for teaching and were professional educators.
  • This distinction set Socrates apart, emphasizing his commitment to truth and inquiry rather than profit.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • Dialectic vs Logic → The passage says Socrates used the dialectic method, but it doesn’t say the Sophists used logic specifically.
  • Focus on youth vs adults → Not mentioned in the passage.
  • Socrates charged high fees → Incorrect; he did not charge at all.

Q- Which of the following best describes Aristotle’s "Empiricism" as mentioned in the text?
option_1: The belief that truth is found only through metaphysical meditation.
option_2: The idea that knowledge is obtained via observation and senses.
option_3: The conviction that the physical world is a shadow of perfect forms.
option_4: The practice of using the dialectic method to reach a conclusion.
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: The idea that knowledge is obtained via observation and senses.

Explanation:

  • The passage states that Aristotle diverged from Plato’s idealism and championed Empiricism.
  • Empiricism means that knowledge is derived from sensory experience and logical categorization.
  • Unlike Plato, who emphasized metaphysical "Forms," Aristotle focused on the empirical world—what can be observed, measured, and classified.
  • This laid the foundation for modern scientific methodology.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • Metaphysical meditation → Refers more to Plato’s philosophy, not Aristotle’s.
  • Shadow of perfect forms → This is Plato’s Theory of Forms, not Aristotle’s empiricism.
  • Dialectic method → Associated with Socrates, not Aristotle.

Q- What does the term "Midwife" signify in the context of Socrates’ teaching style?
option_1: He assisted others in the birth of their own original thoughts.
option_2: He was responsible for the physical well-being of his many students.
option_3: He created new ideas and forced his students to memorize them.
option_4: He delivered lectures in a formal setting like a modern academy.
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: He assisted others in the birth of their own original thoughts.

Explanation:

  • Socrates described himself as a “midwife” of ideas, meaning he did not claim to possess wisdom himself.
  • Instead, through his dialectic method (questioning and dialogue), he helped others discover and refine their own thoughts.
  • This metaphor emphasizes that Socrates guided intellectual development rather than imposing knowledge.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • Physical well-being of students → Not mentioned in the passage.
  • Created new ideas and forced memorization → Opposite of Socratic method; he encouraged independent thinking.
  • Delivered lectures in formal academies → That was more aligned with Plato’s Academy, not Socrates’ informal discussions.

His trial and subsequent execution for impiety and corrupting the youth marked a pivotal moment in Western thought, transforming him into a martyr for the cause of rational inquiry and moral integrity.

Plato, Socrates' most illustrious student, preserved his mentor’s legacy while expanding into metaphysical realms. Q- Based on the passage, how did Plato's "Theory of Forms" view the physical world?
option_1: As the only absolute reality that humans can truly understand.
option_2: As a flawless representation of the ideas found in the heavens.
option_3: As an imperfect reflection of a more perfect and stable reality.
option_4: As a scientific laboratory meant for empirical experimentation.
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: As an imperfect reflection of a more perfect and stable reality.

Explanation:

  • Plato’s Theory of Forms asserts that the physical world is not the ultimate reality.
  • Instead, it is an imperfect, shadowy reflection of a higher, immutable realm of perfect ideas (Forms).
  • For example, a physical chair is only an imperfect copy of the perfect "Form of Chair" that exists in the metaphysical realm.
  • Thus, true knowledge lies not in the physical world but in understanding these eternal Forms.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • Absolute reality humans can understand → This describes Aristotle’s empiricism, not Plato’s idealism.
  • Flawless representation of heavenly ideas → Incorrect; Plato says the physical world is flawed, not flawless.
  • Scientific laboratory for experimentation → This reflects Aristotle’s approach, not Plato’s.

Q- Why did the author mention that Aristotle "looked toward the earth"?
option_1: To suggest he was uninterested in the concept of divine wisdom.

  • Geology and agriculture → Too narrow; his empiricism covered all natural phenomena, not just earth sciences.
  • Felt superior to Plato → The passage doesn’t suggest arrogance, only philosophical divergence.

question:
Arrange the following sentences to form a coherent paragraph:

P: Students spent hours there preparing for their final exams.

Q: Silence was the only rule that everyone strictly followed.

R: Its shelves were packed with books on every imaginable subject.

S: The library was a sanctuary for those seeking knowledge.
option_1: SQPR
option_2: QSRP
option_3: SRQP
option_4: QRPS
solution_text:
Correct Order: S → R → Q → P

Explanation:

  • S introduces the library as a place of knowledge (starting point).
  • R describes the contents of the library (books on all subjects).
  • Q explains the rule of silence, maintaining the environment.
  • P concludes with how students utilised the library.

Final Paragraph

The library was a sanctuary for those seeking knowledge. Its shelves were packed with books on every imaginable subject. Silence was the only rule that everyone strictly followed. Students spent hours there preparing for their final exams.

question:
One Word Substitution:

Science of the races of mankind
option_1: Genealogy
option_2: Ethnology
option_3: Etymology
option_4: Epistemology
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Ethnology (मानव जातियों का विज्ञान/नृविज्ञान)

Explanation

  • Ethnology is the branch of anthropology that studies the races, cultures, and characteristics of mankind (मानव जातियों का विज्ञान/नृविज्ञान) ।
  • Example: Ethnology helps us understand the cultural differences among human races.

Options Recap

  • Genealogy (वंशावली/परिवार वृक्ष) ❌ → Study of family lineage, not races.
  • Ethnology (मानव जातियों का विज्ञान/नृविज्ञान) ✅ → Correct choice.
  • Etymology (शब्दोत्पत्ति विज्ञान) ❌ → Study of origin of words.
  • Epistemology (ज्ञानमीमांसा) ❌ → Study of knowledge and its nature.

question:
Change the narration of the following sentence:

Rahul said, "I am playing cricket with my friends now."
option_1: Rahul said that I was playing cricket with my friends then.
option_2: Rahul said that he is playing cricket with his friends now.
option_3: Rahul said that he was playing cricket with his friends then.
option_4: Rahul said that he has been playing cricket with his friends then.
solution_text:
Answer: (c)

Explanation:

  • The reporting verb “said” is in past tense, so the tense changes: am playing → was playing.
  • Pronoun changes: “I” → “he”, “my” → “his”.
  • Time word changes: “now” → “then”.
  • Hence, the correct answer is Rahul said that he was playing cricket with his friends then.

question:
Improve the highlighted part of the sentence:

Cyber-crime have been in the news since the nineteen eighties.
option_1: has been in the news since
option_2: has been in the news from
option_3: has been on the news since
option_4: No Improvement
solution_text:
Answer: (a)

Explanation:

  • “Cyber-crime” is treated as a singular noun, so it should take “has been”, not “have been”.
  • The correct preposition with duration from past to present is “since”, not “from”.
  • The correct phrase is “in the news”, not “on the news”.
  • Hence, the correct answer is has been in the news since.

question:
Change the narration of the following sentence:

The stranger said to the boy, "Can you tell me the way to the nearest hotel?"
option_1: The stranger enquired the boy that could he tell the way to the nearest hotel.
option_2: The stranger asked the boy if he could tell him the way to the nearest hotel.
option_3: The stranger told the boy to tell him the way to the nearest hotel.
option_4: The stranger asked the boy if he can tell him the way to the nearest hotel.
solution_text:
Answer: (b)

Explanation:

  • The sentence is an interrogative (question), so we use “asked” or “enquired” + if/whether.
  • Since reporting verb is in past (said), “can” → “could”.
  • Pronoun changes: “me” → “him”.
  • Hence, the correct answer is The stranger asked the boy if he could tell him the way to the nearest hotel.

question:
One Word Substitution:

A medicine to nullify the effect of poison.
option_1: Antigen
option_2: Antibiotic
option_3: Antiseptic
option_4: Antidote
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Antidote (विषनाशक/ज़हर का इलाज)

Explanation

  • Antidote means a medicine or substance that counteracts the effects of poison (विषनाशक/ज़हर का इलाज) ।
  • Example: The doctor quickly administered an antidote to save the patient. (डॉक्टर ने तुरंत विषनाशक दवा दी ताकि मरीज को बचाया जा सके) ।

Options Recap

  • Antigen (प्रतिजन) ❌ → A substance that triggers an immune response, not for poison.
  • Antibiotic (प्रतिजैविक) ❌ → Fights bacteria, not poison.
  • Antiseptic (प्रतिजीवाणु) ❌ → Prevents infection, not poison.
  • Antidote (विषनाशक/ज़हर का इलाज) ✅ → Correct choice.

question:
Change the voice of the following sentence:

They were playing cricket in the park.
option_1: Cricket was played in the park by them.
option_2: Cricket was being played in the park by them.
option_3: Cricket is being played in the park by them.
option_4: Cricket had been played in the park by them.
solution_text:
Answer: (b)

Explanation:

  • The given sentence is in past continuous tense (were playing).
  • Passive form of past continuous: was/were being + V3.
  • “were playing” becomes “was being played” in passive voice.
  • Hence, the correct answer is Cricket was being played in the park by them.

question:
Identify the error given in the sentence:

She / can have been / more polite / to him.
option_1: She
option_2: can have been
option_3: more polite
option_4: to him.
solution_text:
Answer: (b)

Explanation:

  • The phrase “can have been” is incorrect in this context.
  • For past possibility/criticism, we use “could have been”, not “can have been”.
  • “Can” is used for present ability, while “could have been” refers to past possibility.
  • Correct sentence: “She could have been more polite to him.”

question: Antonym of WACKY
option_1: Crazy
option_2: Sensible
option_3: Silly
option_4: Absurd
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Sensible (समझदार/विवेकपूर्ण)

Explanation

  • Wacky means silly, crazy, or absurd (पागलपन भरा/मूर्खतापूर्ण/अजीब)।
  • Its antonym would be the opposite quality, i.e., rational, reasonable, or sensible.
  • Sensible (समझदार/विवेकपूर्ण) perfectly contrasts with wacky.

Options Recap

  • Crazy (पागल/अजीब) ❌ → Synonym, not antonym.
  • Sensible (समझदार/विवेकपूर्ण) ✅ → Correct antonym.
  • Silly (मूर्खतापूर्ण) ❌ → Synonym, not antonym.
  • Absurd (बेतुका/अजीब) ❌ → Synonym, not antonym.

question:
Improve the highlighted part of the sentence:

I imagine you have learnt a fruitful lesson from this experience, didn’t you?
option_1: did you?
option_2: have you?
option_3: haven’t you?
option_4: No Improvement
solution_text:
Answer: (c)

Explanation:

  • The main clause is “you have learnt” (present perfect), so the tag should match the auxiliary “have”.
  • A positive statement takes a negative tag → “haven’t you?”
  • “didn’t you?” is incorrect because it relates to simple past, not present perfect.
  • Hence, the correct answer is haven’t you?

question:
IDIOM:

Chicken-hearted
option_1: Kind
option_2: Selfish
option_3: Brave
option_4: Coward
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Coward (कायर/डरपोक)

Explanation

  • Chicken-hearted is an idiom meaning cowardly, lacking courage, easily frightened (कायर/डरपोक/भयभीत)।
  • Example: He was too chicken-hearted to confront the bully. (वह गुंडे का सामना करने के लिए बहुत डरपोक था)।

Options Recap

  • Kind (दयालु) ❌ → Not related.
  • Selfish (स्वार्थी) ❌ → Different meaning.
  • Brave (बहादुर) ❌ → Opposite meaning.
  • Coward (कायर/डरपोक) ✅ → Correct choice.

question:
Identify the error given in the sentence:

You and I / have submitted / your work / on time.
option_1: You and I
option_2: have submitted
option_3: your work
option_4: on time.
solution_text:
Answer: Error in part (c) – “your work”

Explanation:

  • The subject is “You and I” (we), so the possessive pronoun should be “our”, not “your”.
  • “Your” refers only to the second person, which does not match the combined subject.
  • Correct sentence: “You and I have submitted our work on time.”
  • Hence, the error is in part (c).

question:
Change the voice of the following sentence:

The jury reached a unanimous decision.
option_1: A unanimous decision had been reached by the jury.
option_2: A unanimous decision is reached by the jury.
option_3: A unanimous decision has been reached by the jury.
option_4: A unanimous decision was reached by the jury.
solution_text:
Answer: (d)

Explanation:

  • The given sentence is in simple past tense (reached).
  • Passive voice of simple past is: was/were + V3 (past participle).
  • So, “reached” becomes “was reached” in passive form.
  • Hence, the correct answer is A unanimous decision was reached by the jury.

question: Antonym of CHRONIC
option_1: Temporary
option_2: Persistent
option_3: Constant
option_4: Regular
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Temporary (अस्थायी/क्षणिक)

Explanation

  • Chronic means long-lasting, persistent, continuing for a long time (usually used for diseases or problems) (दीर्घकालिक/लंबे समय तक रहने वाला)।
  • Its antonym would be something short-term or not lasting long.
  • Temporary (अस्थायी/क्षणिक) is the correct opposite.

Options Recap

  • Temporary (अस्थायी/क्षणिक) ✅ → Correct antonym.
  • Persistent (लगातार/स्थायी) ❌ → Synonym, not antonym.
  • Constant (निरंतर/लगातार) ❌ → Synonym, not antonym.
  • Regular (नियमित) ❌ → Related but not opposite.

question:
Arrange the following sentences to form a coherent paragraph:

P: Such a move would significantly reduce carbon emissions.

Q: The government is planning to replace old buses with electric ones.

R: This initiative is part of a larger plan to combat air pollution.

S: Citizens have welcomed this step with great enthusiasm.
option_1: QRSP
option_2: SQRP
option_3: QPRS
option_4: SRPQ
solution_text:
Q → P → R → S

Explanation:

  • Q introduces the main idea (government plan).
  • P explains the impact of that move (reducing carbon emissions).
  • R connects the initiative to a broader plan (air pollution control).
  • S concludes with public reaction.

Final Paragraph

The government is planning to replace old buses with electric ones. Such a move would significantly reduce carbon emissions. This initiative is part of a larger plan to combat air pollution. Citizens have welcomed this step with great enthusiasm.

question:
Fill in the Blank:

I am going to ________ a letter to my friend.
option_1: rite
option_2: wright
option_3: right
option_4: write
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Write (लिखना)

Explanation

  • The sentence is: I am going to ________ a letter to my friend.
  • Write means to compose or put words on paper (लिखना)।
  • Example: I will write a letter to my friend tomorrow. (मैं कल अपने मित्र को पत्र लिखूँगा) ।

Options Recap

  • Rite (अनुष्ठान) ❌ → Refers to a religious ceremony, not suitable.
  • Wright (निर्माता/कारीगर) ❌ → Means a worker or maker (e.g., playwright, shipwright).
  • Right (सही/अधिकार) ❌ → Refers to correctness or entitlement, not suitable.
  • Write (लिखना) ✅ → Correct choice.

question: Synonym of QUASH
option_1: Support
option_2: Reject
option_3: Create
option_4: Essential
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Reject (अस्वीकार करना/रद्द करना)

Explanation

  • Quash means to suppress, reject, or put an end to something, especially legally or formally (रद्द करना/अस्वीकार करना/दबाना) ।
  • Example: The judge quashed the petition due to lack of evidence. (न्यायाधीश ने साक्ष्य की कमी के कारण याचिका को रद्द कर दिया) ।

Options Recap

  • Support (समर्थन करना) ❌ → Opposite meaning.
  • Reject (अस्वीकार करना/रद्द करना) ✅ → Correct synonym.
  • Create (सृजन करना) ❌ → Different meaning.
  • Essential (आवश्यक) ❌ → Not related.

question: Which Vedic text is known as the 'Book of Chants'?
option_1: Rig Veda
option_2: Yajur Veda
option_3: Sama Veda
option_4: Atharva Veda
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Option: Sama Veda

Explanation:

  • The Sama Veda is known as the “Book of Chants” because it primarily consists of hymns taken from the Rig Veda, but arranged in a way suitable for chanting during rituals.
  • It focuses on the musical rendering of Vedic hymns, forming the foundation of Indian classical music traditions.
  • Priests used the Sama Veda during sacrificial ceremonies, where chanting was essential.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • Rig Veda → Known as the “Book of Hymns,” contains the oldest Vedic hymns.
  • Yajur Veda → Known as the “Book of Rituals,” focuses on sacrificial formulas.
  • Atharva Veda → Known as the “Book of Spells/Knowledge,” deals with everyday life, charms, and healing.

question: Which Amendment Act of the Constitution of India increased the elective strength of the Lok Sabha from 525 to 545?
option_1: 31st Amendment Act
option_2: 42nd Amendment Act
option_3: 44th Amendment Act
option_4: 52nd Amendment Act
solution_text:
The elective strength of the Lok Sabha was increased from 525 to 545 members by the 31st Amendment Act, 1973.

Explanation:

  • The 31st Constitutional Amendment Act, 1973 was enacted to adjust representation in the Lok Sabha and State Assemblies in line with population changes.
  • Before this amendment, the elective strength of the Lok Sabha was 525.
  • After the amendment, it was raised to 545, ensuring better representation of citizens in Parliament.
  • This change reflected India’s growing population and the need for proportional representation.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • 42nd Amendment Act (1976) → Known as the “Mini Constitution,” it made sweeping changes but did not alter Lok Sabha strength.
  • 44th Amendment Act (1978) → Restored democratic provisions after the Emergency, but did not deal with Lok Sabha numbers.
  • 52nd Amendment Act (1985) → Introduced the Anti-Defection Law, unrelated to Lok Sabha strength.

question: The 'Black Sea' does NOT share a coastline with which of the following countries?
option_1: Turkey
option_2: Georgia
option_3: Bulgaria
option_4: Italy
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Italy

Explanation:

  • The Black Sea is bordered by six countries:
    1. Turkey
    2. Georgia
    3. Russia
    4. Ukraine
    5. Romania
    6. Bulgaria
  • Italy does not share a coastline with the Black Sea. It is located further west, along the Mediterranean Sea.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • Turkey → Has a long Black Sea coastline.
  • Georgia → Lies on the eastern coast of the Black Sea.
  • Bulgaria → Lies on the western coast of the Black Sea.

question:
Consider the following statements regarding Article 39A of the Indian Constitution:

1. It was explicitly incorporated into the Constitution through the 44th Constitutional Amendment Act.

2. It mandates the State to ensure equal justice and provide free legal aid to citizens facing economic or social disadvantages.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
option_1: 2 only
option_2: 1 only
option_3: Both 1 and 2
option_4: Neither 1 nor 2
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: 2 only

Explanation:

  • Article 39A was introduced by the 42nd Constitutional Amendment Act, 1976.
  • It directs the State to ensure equal justice and provide free legal aid so that justice is not denied to any citizen due to economic or social disadvantage.
  • This provision is part of the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSPs) and emphasizes the importance of a fair legal system accessible to all.

question: In 2025, the 'Anusandhan National Research Foundation' (ANRF) became fully operational. It replaces which body?
option_1: CSIR
option_2: ASRB
option_3: SERB
option_4: ICAR
solution_text:
The Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF), which became fully operational in 2025, replaced the Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB). It was established under the ANRF Act, 2023, as the apex body to steer India’s research and development landscape.

Key Details:

  • Previous Body Replaced:
    • Science and Engineering Research Board (SERB), created in 2008, was subsumed into ANRF.
  • Establishment:
    • ANRF was set up under the ANRF Act, 2023.
  • Operational Year:
    • Became fully operational in 2025.
  • Parent Department:
    • Functions under the Department of Science and Technology (DST).
  • Mandate:
    • To provide high-level strategic direction for scientific research in India.
    • To seed, grow, and promote R&D across universities, colleges, labs, and private institutions.
    • To align with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 goals of strengthening research culture.

question:
Consider the following statements:

1.Nokrek was officially designated as a biosphere reserve in the year 1988.

2. The reserve serves as a habitat for rare and endangered fauna, including the red panda and elephants.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
option_1: 1 only
option_2: Both 1 and 2
option_3: 2 only
option_4: Neither 1 nor 2
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Both 1 and 2

Explanation

  • The Nokrek Biosphere Reserve is located in the West Garo Hills district of Meghalaya.
  • It is part of the Garo Hills and is famous for its rich biodiversity, including rare orchids, medicinal plants, and wild citrus species (considered the genetic origin of citrus fruits).
  • It is also home to species like the red panda, elephants, and a variety of birds.
  • Nokrek was declared a biosphere reserve in 1988 and is recognized under UNESCO’s Man and the Biosphere Programme.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • Arunachal Pradesh → Has biosphere reserves like Dihang-Dibang, not Nokrek.
  • Sikkim → Known for Khangchendzonga Biosphere Reserve.
  • Nagaland → Does not host Nokrek Biosphere Reserve.

question: The 'Tertiary Sector' of the Indian economy includes:
option_1: Agriculture
option_2: Mining
option_3: Manufacturing
option_4: Banking
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Banking

Explanation:

  • The Indian economy is divided into three sectors:
    1. Primary Sector → Agriculture, forestry, fishing, mining (raw material extraction).
    2. Secondary Sector → Manufacturing, construction, industries (processing and production).
    3. Tertiary Sector → Services such as banking, insurance, transport, communication, trade, education, health, tourism, IT, etc.
  • The Tertiary Sector is also called the Service Sector, and it contributes the largest share to India’s GDP today.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • Agriculture → Belongs to the Primary Sector.
  • Mining → Part of the Primary Sector.
  • Manufacturing → Belongs to the Secondary Sector.

question: Which acid is present in 'Stinging Nettle' leaves?
option_1: Citric Acid
option_2: Formic Acid
option_3: Oxalic Acid
option_4: Tartaric Acid
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Formic Acid

Explanation:

  • The stinging hairs (trichomes) of Stinging Nettle (Urtica dioica) inject formic acid (methanoic acid) along with histamine and other irritants into the skin.
  • This causes the characteristic burning, itching, and redness when touched.
  • Formic acid is the same compound found in ant stings, which is why the sensation is similar.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • Citric Acid → Found in citrus fruits like lemon and orange.
  • Oxalic Acid → Present in spinach and rhubarb, but not the main irritant in nettles.
  • Tartaric Acid → Found in grapes and tamarind.

question: The 'Shore Temple' at Mahabalipuram was built during the reign of which dynasty?
option_1: Chola
option_2: Chera
option_3: Pallava
option_4: Pandya
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Pallava

Explanation:

  • The Shore Temple at Mahabalipuram (Mamallapuram) was built during the reign of the Pallava dynasty in the 8th century CE.
  • It was commissioned by Narasimhavarman II (Rajasimha) and is one of the earliest structural stone temples in South India.
  • The temple is dedicated to Lord Shiva and Lord Vishnu, showcasing the Pallava’s architectural brilliance.
  • It is part of the UNESCO World Heritage Site – Group of Monuments at Mahabalipuram.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • Chola → Famous for later Dravidian temples like Brihadeeswara (Thanjavur), not Mahabalipuram.
  • Chera → Associated with Kerala region, not Mahabalipuram.
  • Pandya → Known for Madurai and Meenakshi temple, not the Shore Temple.

question: The resistance of an ideal ammeter should be:
option_1: Zero
option_2: Infinite
option_3: Very High
option_4: Very Low
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Zero

Explanation:

  • An ammeter is used to measure current in a circuit.
  • To measure current accurately, it must be connected in series with the circuit.
  • If the ammeter had resistance, it would alter the current flowing through the circuit.
  • Therefore, the resistance of an ideal ammeter is zero, so it does not affect the circuit at all.

question: 'Bibi Ka Maqbara' is a replica of the Taj Mahal located in:
option_1: Bhopal
option_2: Aurangabad
option_3: Lucknow
option_4: Hyderabad
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Aurangabad

Explanation:

  • Bibi Ka Maqbara is a mausoleum located in Aurangabad, Maharashtra.
  • It was commissioned by Aurangzeb’s son, Azam Shah, in memory of his mother Dilras Banu Begum.
  • Because of its striking resemblance to the Taj Mahal in Agra, it is often called the “Taj of the Deccan.”
  • The monument was built in the late 17th century (1678–1679) and is one of the most famous Mughal-era structures in the Deccan region.

question:
With reference to the rivers associated with the Kumbh Mela venues, consider the following statements:

1. At Prayagraj, the festival takes place at the confluence of the Ganga, Yamuna, and mythical Saraswati rivers.

2. The holy gatherings at Ujjain and Nasik are organized on the banks of the Shipra and Godavari rivers, respectively.

Which of the statements given above is/are Incorrect?
option_1: 1 only
option_2: 2 only
option_3: Neither 1 nor 2
option_4: Both 1 and 2
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Neither 1 nor 2

Explanation:

  • The Kumbh Mela is one of the largest religious gatherings in the world, celebrated at four locations in India:
    1. Prayagraj (Allahabad) – at the confluence of Ganga, Yamuna, and mythical Saraswati.
    2. Haridwar – on the banks of the Ganga.
    3. Ujjain – on the banks of the Shipra river.
    4. Nasik – on the banks of the Godavari river.
  • Varanasi, though a major Hindu pilgrimage site, is not a Kumbh Mela venue. It is famous for its ghats, temples, and spiritual heritage, but the Kumbh Mela is not organized there.

question: The 'UDAN' scheme is associated with:
option_1: Higher education loans.
option_2: Regional airport connectivity.
option_3: Skill development for youth.
option_4: Women startup funding.
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Regional airport connectivity

Explanation:

  • The UDAN scheme (Ude Desh ka Aam Naagrik) was launched in 2016 under the Ministry of Civil Aviation.
  • Its aim is to make air travel affordable and widespread by enhancing regional airport connectivity.
  • The scheme focuses on:
    • Reviving under-served and unserved airports.
    • Providing affordable flight fares (capped at ₹2,500 for a one-hour flight).
    • Boosting economic growth and tourism in smaller towns and cities.

question: The 'Atmospheric layer' that reflects radio waves back to Earth is:
option_1: Stratosphere
option_2: Mesosphere
option_3: Ionosphere
option_4: Exosphere
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Ionosphere

Explanation:

  • The Ionosphere is the atmospheric layer that contains a high concentration of ionized particles.
  • These charged particles can reflect and refract radio waves back to Earth, making long-distance radio communication possible without satellites.
  • It lies roughly between 50 km to 1,000 km above Earth’s surface, overlapping parts of the mesosphere and thermosphere.
  • This property of the ionosphere is crucial for shortwave radio broadcasting, amateur radio, and military communication systems.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • Stratosphere → Contains the ozone layer, but does not reflect radio waves.
  • Mesosphere → Known for meteor burning, not radio reflection.
  • Exosphere → Outermost layer, where particles escape into space, not suitable for radio reflection.

question: The 'Objective Resolution', which formed the basis of the Preamble, was moved in the Constituent Assembly by:
option_1: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar
option_2: Dr. Rajendra Prasad
option_3: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel
option_4: Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru

Explanation:

  • The Objective Resolution was moved in the Constituent Assembly on 13 December 1946 by Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru.
  • It laid down the guiding principles and philosophy for the framing of the Indian Constitution.
  • The Resolution declared that:
    • India would be an independent sovereign republic.
    • The Constitution would guarantee justice, equality, and freedom to all citizens.
    • Adequate safeguards would be provided for minorities, backward classes, and tribal areas.
  • This Resolution later became the basis of the Preamble to the Constitution of India.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • Dr. B.R. Ambedkar → Chairman of the Drafting Committee, but did not move the Objective Resolution.
  • Dr. Rajendra Prasad → President of the Constituent Assembly, presided over the sessions.
  • Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel → Played a key role in integration of princely states, not in moving the Resolution.

question: The energy stored in a stretched rubber band is:
option_1: Magnetic Energy
option_2: Chemical Energy
option_3: Potential Energy
option_4: Kinetic Energy
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Potential Energy

Explanation:

  • When a rubber band is stretched, work is done against the elastic force of the band.
  • This work gets stored in the form of elastic potential energy.
  • Once released, the stored potential energy is converted into kinetic energy as the band snaps back to its original shape.

question: Which committee recommended the establishment of Regional Rural Banks (RRBs)?
option_1: Narasimham Committee
option_2: Shivraman Committee
option_3: Gadgil Committee
option_4: Rangarajan Committee
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Narasimham Committee

Explanation:

  • The Narasimham Working Group (1975) recommended the establishment of Regional Rural Banks (RRBs) in India.
  • The idea was to create banks that combined the local feel and rural orientation of cooperatives with the business organization and professionalism of commercial banks.
  • As a result, the first RRBs were set up in 1975, with the aim of providing credit and financial services to rural areas, especially to small and marginal farmers, agricultural laborers, and rural artisans.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • Shivraman Committee → Focused on agricultural reforms, not RRBs.
  • Gadgil Committee → Known for its role in formulating the Five-Year Plans, not RRBs.
  • Rangarajan Committee → Focused on banking sector reforms and computerization, not the creation of RRBs.

question: The 'Digha Nikaya' is a famous collection of long discourses in which of the following?
option_1: Jain Agamas
option_2: Vedic Literature
option_3: Buddhist Canon
option_4: Sangam Literature
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Buddhist Canon

Explanation:

  • The Digha Nikaya is one of the five collections (Nikayas) in the Sutta Pitaka, which is part of the Tripitaka (Buddhist Canon).
  • It contains long discourses of the Buddha, covering topics like ethics, meditation, cosmology, and philosophical debates.
  • The term Digha means "long," and Nikaya means "collection," hence it is a compilation of long discourses.
  • These texts are preserved in Pali language and form a core part of Theravada Buddhism’s scriptures.

question: 'Kalaripayattu' is a traditional martial art form from which state?
option_1: Kerala
option_2: Tamil Nadu
option_3: Andhra Pradesh
option_4: Karnataka
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Kerala

Explanation:

  • Kalaripayattu is one of the oldest martial art forms in the world, originating in Kerala.
  • It dates back over 3,000 years and is deeply rooted in the traditions of the state.
  • The art involves strikes, kicks, grappling, weaponry, and healing techniques.
  • It is often described as the mother of all martial arts, with influences seen in Asian martial traditions.
  • Today, Kalaripayattu is practiced both as a martial discipline and as a performance art, often showcased in Kerala’s cultural festivals.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • Tamil Nadu → Known for Silambam (stick fighting), not Kalaripayattu.
  • Andhra Pradesh → Has Kathi Samu and other local martial traditions, but not Kalaripayattu.
  • Karnataka → Known for Garadi Mane wrestling traditions, not Kalaripayattu.

question: Who appoints the State Election Commissioner?
option_1: The President
option_2: The Governor
option_3: The Chief Minister
option_4: The Chief Justice of High Court
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Option: The Governor

Explanation:

  • According to Article 243K of the Indian Constitution, the State Election Commissioner is appointed by the Governor of the State.
  • The State Election Commission is responsible for conducting elections to the Panchayats and Municipalities within the state.
  • While the Governor makes the appointment, the State Election Commissioner enjoys independence similar to the Chief Election Commissioner of India, as they cannot be removed except in the manner and grounds prescribed for a High Court judge.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • President → Appoints the Chief Election Commissioner of India, not the State Election Commissioner.
  • Chief Minister → Has no constitutional role in this appointment.
  • Chief Justice of High Court → Not involved in the appointment process.

question: Which of the following straits connects the Red Sea and the Gulf of Aden?
option_1: Strait of Malacca
option_2: Strait of Gibraltar
option_3: Bab-el-Mandeb
option_4: Strait of Hormuz
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Option: Bab-el-Mandeb

Explanation:

  • The Bab-el-Mandeb Strait connects the Red Sea to the Gulf of Aden, and further to the Arabian Sea.
  • It is a strategically important waterway, often referred to as the “Gate of Tears”, because of its narrow passage and historical dangers for sailors.
  • This strait is crucial for global trade, especially oil shipments, as it forms part of the route linking the Mediterranean Sea (via Suez Canal and Red Sea) to the Indian Ocean.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • Strait of Malacca → Connects the Andaman Sea (Indian Ocean) with the South China Sea (Pacific Ocean).
  • Strait of Gibraltar → Connects the Atlantic Ocean with the Mediterranean Sea.
  • Strait of Hormuz → Connects the Persian Gulf with the Gulf of Oman and Arabian Sea.

question: 'Double Fertilization' is a characteristic feature of:
option_1: Algae
option_2: Bryophytes
option_3: Pteridophytes
option_4: Angiosperms
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Option: Angiosperms

Explanation:

  • Double fertilization is a unique and defining feature of angiosperms (flowering plants).
  • In this process:
    1. One male gamete fuses with the egg cell → forming a diploid zygote (2n).
    2. The other male gamete fuses with two polar nuclei → forming a triploid endosperm (3n), which nourishes the developing embryo.
  • This phenomenon ensures efficient utilization of male gametes and provides a nutritive tissue (endosperm) for seed development.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • Algae → Fertilization occurs, but no double fertilization.
  • Bryophytes → Show alternation of generations, but fertilization is single.
  • Pteridophytes → Fertilization is water-dependent, not double.

question: The 2007 Supreme Court ruling in the I.R. Coelho case established the legal principle that:
option_1: Fundamental rights are secondary to all Directive Principles.
option_2: Executive actions are exempt from any form of judicial review.
option_3: Presidential assent is mandatory for all state property laws.
option_4: Ninth Schedule laws are subject to the basic structure test.
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Ninth Schedule laws are subject to the basic structure test.

Explanation:

  • In the I.R. Coelho vs State of Tamil Nadu (2007) case, the Supreme Court ruled that laws placed under the Ninth Schedule of the Constitution are not immune from judicial review.
  • The Court held that even if a law is included in the Ninth Schedule, it can still be struck down if it violates the “basic structure” of the Constitution (a principle established in the Kesavananda Bharati case, 1973).
  • This judgment reinforced the supremacy of the Constitution and ensured that Parliament cannot bypass fundamental rights by simply placing laws in the Ninth Schedule.

question: The 'Hindustan Socialist Republican Association' (HSRA) was founded in 1928 at which city?
option_1: Kapur
option_2: Delhi
option_3: Allahabad
option_4: Lahore
solution_text:
✅ Correct Answer: Delhi

 Explanation:

  • The Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (HSRA) was founded in 1928 at Feroz Shah Kotla, Delhi.
  • It was established by revolutionaries including Bhagat Singh, Chandrashekhar Azad, Sukhdev, and others, after reorganizing the earlier Hindustan Republican Association (HRA).
  • The HSRA aimed to overthrow British colonial rule through revolutionary activities and inspire youth with socialist ideals.
  • Its formation marked a significant shift from purely nationalist revolution to a broader vision of socialist transformation in India.

❌ Why other options are wrong

  • Kapur → Not historically linked to HSRA’s foundation.
  • Allahabad → Associated with the earlier HRA (1924), but HSRA was founded later in Delhi.
  • Lahore → Important in revolutionary activities (e.g., Bhagat Singh’s trial), but not the founding place.

12 ÷ 3 × 4 + 18 − 6 = 28

LHS = 12 ÷ 3 × 4 + 18 − 6

question:

Some papers are files.

Conclusions:
I. All papers are books.
II. Some books are files.
option_1: Only conclusion I follows.
option_2: Only conclusion II follows.
option_3: Both conclusion I and II follows.
option_4: Neither conclusion I nor II follows.
solution_text:
Conclusion I: All papers are books ❌

  • Reverse of the statement
  • From Books ⊂ Papers, we cannot say Papers ⊂ Books

Conclusion II: Some books are files ❌

  • We only know some papers are files
  • But books may or may not be in that “some” part

Final Answer: Neither conclusion I nor II follows ✔

question:
In the following question, one statement is given, followed by certain inferences. Consider the statement to be true even if it seems to contradict commonly known facts. Read the inferences carefully and decide which of them logically follows from the given statement.

Statement:
Machines can perform repetitive tasks faster and more accurately than humans.

Inferences:
I. Machines are more efficient than humans in repetitive tasks.
II. Humans and machines differ in their capabilities.
option_1: Only I follows
option_2: Neither I nor II follows
option_3: Only II follows
option_4: Both I and II follow
solution_text:
👉 Inference I:
Since machines are both faster and more accurate, they complete repetitive tasks better than humans. Efficiency means doing work in less time with better accuracy. Therefore, machines are more efficient in repetitive tasks.
✔ Inference I follows

👉 Inference II:
The statement compares humans and machines and shows that machines perform better in certain tasks. This clearly indicates that both do not have the same ability, i.e., there is a difference between them.
✔ Inference II follows

👉 Final Answer: Both I and II follow
